model_narrative: We route a single commodity through a directed network from one source node to a set of demand nodes over a fixed set of existing arcs. For each arc we decide how much flow to send across it and whether to open the arc at all. Sending flow incurs a variable cost per unit carried, and opening an arc incurs a separate one-time fixed cost that is charged whenever the arc is used. The objective is to minimize the total cost, combining the variable flow cost over all arcs with the fixed opening cost of every arc that is opened.

sets.name: n
sets.doc: the network nodes; one distinguished node acts as the single source that supplies the commodity and the others are demand nodes that consume it

sets.name: s
sets.doc: the sub-arc label distinguishing parallel arcs between the same ordered pair of nodes; here there is a single label so at most one arc runs in each direction between any two nodes

sets.name: arc
sets.doc: the existing directed arcs over which flow may be routed, each identified by an origin node, a destination node, and a sub-arc label; this is a sparse three-dimensional set listing only the arcs that physically exist

params.name: vcost
params.doc: the variable cost charged per unit of flow sent over an arc, a single scalar that applies uniformly to every arc

params.name: fcost
params.doc: the one-time fixed cost incurred for opening an arc, charged in full whenever the arc carries any flow, regardless of how much

params.name: xupp
params.doc: the maximum amount of flow an arc may carry, equal to the total supply available at the source so that a single arc could in principle carry the entire commodity

params.name: demand
params.doc: the net demand of each node, defined as consumption minus production; it is positive at demand nodes, negative at the single source node where the magnitude equals the total supply, and zero at pure transshipment nodes

vars.name: x
vars.doc: the amount of flow routed over each arc, a continuous nonnegative quantity

vars.name: y
vars.doc: the open/closed decision for each arc, equal to 1 when the arc is opened and incurs its fixed cost and 0 when it stays closed

constraints.name: x[m,
constraints.intent: At every node the flow must balance so that the total amount arriving minus the total amount leaving equals that node's net demand. For each node, add up the flow on all arcs coming into it, subtract the flow on all arcs going out of it, and require the result to match the net demand of that node, which draws flow toward the demand nodes and out of the source.

constraints.name: x[m,
constraints.intent: No flow may travel over an arc unless that arc has been opened, and even an opened arc cannot carry more than its capacity. For each arc, the flow on it must stay at or below its capacity when the arc is open, and must be forced to zero whenever the arc is closed.

query: We're weighing a single-feeder design where the depot commits to just one outbound line and everything fans out from there. If n1 is allowed to open at most one arc leaving it, what total cost does the model come back with?
expected_pyomo: model.single_feeder = Constraint(expr=sum(model.y['n1', n, 'a1'] for n in model.n if ('n1', n, 'a1') in model.arc) <= 1)
